metadata.name: keeper_book
metadata.description: Keeper — on-chain market-maker book with proof-gated settlement via TxLINE Merkle proofs

instructions.name: close_book
docs: Close an UNSETTLED book, returning rent to the authority. Exists for
operational resets (e.g. a book anchored by a run that was later
fixed) — and deliberately refuses settled books: a proven outcome,
once recorded, is permanent.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Only the book's authority may close it; rent returns to them.

instructions.name: init_book
docs: Open the on-chain book for one fixture. The payer becomes the only
wallet that can record epochs; settlement stays permissionless.

accounts.name: authority
docs: The maker. Pays rent and becomes the book's epoch-recording authority.

instructions.name: record_epoch
docs: Anchor one blotter batch: Merkle root over engine events
`[seq_start, seq_end)` plus the maker's inventory and mark-to-market
P&L at the end of the batch (stake units ×1e6).
The continuity gate is the on-chain guarantee that the anchored
blotter has no gaps: each epoch must begin exactly where the previous
one ended (`seq_start == stored seq_end`, which starts at 0), so the
maker cannot quietly skip a range of events and keep anchoring.
Deliberately still allowed AFTER settlement: the audit trail may keep
appending (post-final P&L snapshots flush late), while the settlement
verdict — proven_goals / winner / status — is written exactly once by
`settle_book` and never touched here. Rejecting late epochs would only
create a liveness footgun (an anchorer retrying a range forever), not
security.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Only the maker that opened the book may extend its anchored history.

instructions.name: settle_book
docs: Settle the book against TxLINE's proven final score. PERMISSIONLESS:
nothing the caller supplies can steer the outcome —
- the payload either chains to TxLINE's own on-chain daily root
(checked by TxLINE itself, via CPI) or the whole tx aborts;
- the gates below pin WHICH proof is acceptable: this fixture's
(fixture bind), final whistle only (finality), the goal stats and
nothing else (stat-key bind), under the expected day's root
(epoch window);
- the winner is computed from the proven leaf values, not from any
argument.
So the "worst" a malicious caller can do is settle the book correctly.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Permissionless caller — pays the fee, controls nothing else.

accounts.name: txline_roots
docs: TxLINE's daily scores Merkle-root account for `epoch_day`.
TXORACLE_ID (never our own program id — the classic foreign-PDA bug)
and requires TxLINE ownership. A wrong-but-well-derived day simply
fails proof validation inside TxLINE.

accounts.name: txline_program
docs: CPI target is executable.

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: signer is not the book authority

errors.name: ContinuityBroken
errors.msg: record_epoch seq_start does not equal the stored seq_end — blotter continuity broken

errors.name: EmptyEpochRange
errors.msg: record_epoch range is empty (seq_end must be > seq_start)

errors.name: EpochDayOutOfRange
errors.msg: epoch_day must be the book's stored day or the next day

errors.name: FixtureMismatch
errors.msg: proof is for a different fixture than this book

errors.name: StatKeysMismatch
errors.msg: proof leaves must be exactly the goal stat keys [1, 2], in order

errors.name: ProofNotFinal
errors.msg: proof leaf is not from the game_finalised record (period != 100)

errors.name: InvalidRootsAccount
errors.msg: txline_roots is not the daily_scores_roots PDA for this epoch_day

errors.name: InvalidTxlineProgram
errors.msg: txline_program is not the TxLINE txoracle program

errors.name: SettledBookIsPermanent
errors.msg: settled books are permanent — refusing to close

types.name: BookSettled
docs: Emitted exactly once per book, when a TxLINE proof settles it.

fields.name: proven_goals
docs: Proven final [P1 goals, P2 goals].

fields.name: winner
docs: WINNER_P1 | WINNER_DRAW | WINNER_P2.

fields.name: epoch_day
docs: Epoch-day whose TxLINE root the proof verified against.

fields.name: proof_ts
docs: Timestamp of the proven score record (ms epoch, from the proof).

types.name: BookState
docs: One market-maker book per fixture. PDA: `["book", fixture_id i64 LE]`.

fields.name: authority
docs: The maker's wallet — the only signer allowed to record epochs.

fields.name: fixture_id
docs: TxLINE fixture id (i64 to match TxLINE's own encoding).

fields.name: epoch_day
docs: Kickoff epoch-day (UTC days since epoch). Settlement accepts proofs
under this day's root or the next (evening kickoffs finalise past

fields.name: p1_is_home
docs: TxLINE `Participant1IsHome` — maps proven P1/P2 goals to home/away.

fields.name: epoch_count
docs: Number of epochs recorded so far.

fields.name: latest_root
docs: Merkle root of the most recent anchored blotter batch.

fields.name: seq_end
docs: EXCLUSIVE end of the anchored seq range == the next expected
`seq_start` == total events anchored. Storing the exclusive bound makes
the continuity check a single equality (`seq_start == seq_end`) with no
first-epoch special case.

fields.name: inventory_micro
docs: Signed inventory per outcome [home, draw, away], stake units ×1e6.

fields.name: mtm_pnl_micro
docs: Mark-to-market P&L at the last recorded epoch, stake units ×1e6.

fields.name: status
docs: STATUS_OPEN | STATUS_SETTLED.

fields.name: proven_goals
docs: Final [P1 goals, P2 goals] proven by TxLINE. Meaningful once settled.

fields.name: winner
docs: WINNER_P1 | WINNER_DRAW | WINNER_P2, WINNER_UNSET until settled.

types.name: EpochRecorded
docs: Emitted once per anchored blotter batch. Together with the on-chain
continuity gate this forms a gap-free, queryable audit trail: replaying a
book's EpochRecorded events reconstructs the full anchored history, and
`scripts/verify-anchors.ts` checks each event's root against a local
recomputation from the blotter.

fields.name: seq_start
docs: Inclusive start of the anchored seq range.

fields.name: seq_end
docs: EXCLUSIVE end of the anchored seq range.

types.name: ProofNode
docs: One sibling hash in a Merkle path.

types.name: ScoreStat
docs: A single provable key/value statistic — the innermost Merkle leaf.
`period` is the match-status period of the underlying score record
(3 = halftime, 100 = game finalised), NOT the stat key's period prefix.

types.name: ScoresBatchSummary
docs: Fixture-level summary — the node that binds a proof to ONE fixture.
`fixture_id` living inside the proven chain is what makes on-chain fixture
binding possible (the FixtureMismatch gate below).

types.name: StatValidationInput
docs: Full `validate_stat_v2` payload (TxLINE IDL: `StatValidationInput`),
exactly as the stat-validation endpoint hands it to the settler. Borsh
round-trips are canonical: deserializing the caller-built payload and
re-serializing it here yields identical bytes, so the proof the caller
fetched is the proof TxLINE verifies.
